name: Depth-First Search
def: Explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking, via stack/recursion.
problem: Traverse, detect cycles, topologically order, and find components/bridges in graphs.
mechanism: Recurse (or use an explicit stack) into an unvisited neighbour until stuck, then backtrack; entry/exit times power many derived algorithms.
benefits: O(V+E); low memory on sparse graphs
Foundation for topo-sort, SCC, cycle detection
limits: Recursion depth → stack overflow on deep graphs
failures: Stack overflow on deep/large graphs (use explicit stack)
Missing visited set → infinite loops on cycles
myths: "DFS finds shortest paths" — it does not in general
